idea-plugin.name: Claude/Codex Sub Autocomplete
idea-plugin.description:
Native IntelliJ autocomplete using the Claude Code or Codex subscription you already have—no API keys or separate autocomplete plan.

  • Switch explicitly between Claude Code and Codex and select a subscription model.
  • Generate expressions, multiline blocks, and comment-directed implementations as native ghost text.
  • Use bounded language-aware editor context, with recent-edit, open-tab, and cross-file sources separately opt-in.
  • Inspect connection state, request progress, safe errors, and latency from the status bar and diagnostics window.
  • Use manual completion by default or opt into automatic requests.

Requests go directly through the selected provider's installed CLI. The plugin does not operate a proxy or telemetry service. Provider tools, project inspection, file writes, and command execution are disabled. Common credential patterns are redacted before requests are sent.

Requirements: IntelliJ IDEA Community or Ultimate 2026.1 or newer, Java 21, and an installed and authenticated Claude Code or Codex CLI.

Claude Code and Codex use agent models rather than purpose-built fill-in-the-middle models. Uncached suggestions can take several seconds, and quality depends on the selected model and context.

This is an independent project and is not affiliated with Anthropic, OpenAI, or JetBrains.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
0.5.7 — Early Access

  • Rejects provider explanations such as “the YAML structure is already complete” instead of rendering them as ghost text.
  • Adds a release-blocking safety matrix for every exposed Claude model and GPT model/reasoning combination.
  • Retains legitimate natural-language YAML scalar completions while filtering provider meta-responses before validation, caching, or rendering.

action.description: Request an inline completion from the selected subscription provider
Request a read-only related-edit proposal from the selected subscription provider
